text:
Horrible experience with T-Mobile since they took over Sprint network. Cell service is not nearly good. Constantly dropping calls, voicemail and text messages are delayed by hours and sometimes days later. 
I was forced into purchasing 5 new phones because T-Mobile was dropping the Sprint 3G network. I was promised a $400 discount for one of the 5 phones, but 10 months later I get the run-around and now they say they are not going to honor the agreement. T-Mobile rep hung up on me before I could say another word after he told me that.

I've been attempting to get my service corrected. I added a line on October 4 th 2022. I was supposed to get a free I phone on T -Mobile with promotion P-135.
I spoke with Customer care so may times I can barley count . Amy, Charlie & Jennifer are a just of a few that said we will call you back and that tru as t me it will be corrected. It has not. I will continue to call but will also use every tool I have to get some one that will fix this issue. 
Don't use T-Mobile, customer care is not helpful.if you want to help call me 5164492536

Tmobile has been the worlds diabolical trashy services in the whole century. They overcharge your bill...Does not know what customer service is...Has the crappiest tower services...does not know how to fix people's problems...and what's worst is they do not care...I would hope TMobile gets what they deserve!!!

Supervisor, Nakiya Robinson did a great job today!  The professional and diligent attention given to each customer is remarkable!  

I bought the wrong phone in hopes she would get it working.  She worked on it for FOUR HOURS! Her patience is to be commended.  Gotta love a dedicated employee!  I will return with the correct phone next time and look forward to having her assist me again.

I called to do a simple thing; add additional data to my plan.  Two hours and forty-eight minutes, three phone calls and 5 people later, they determined they couldn’t do it unless I dropped to a plan with a third of the data I have, waited a month, and paid twice as much.  Unbelievable!!!!!!

Don't go to the t-mobile store on power rd. Mesa, Az
I went there on Wednesday had a new phone
they don't seem to help you.  Battery issues they
didn't nothing to fix the problem

After being overcharged for equipment we did not have, and insurance on devices that did not pay out, T-Mobile gave a $20.00 credit to "re-imburse me" for over $130 dollars of charges. They do not care. They will not transfer you to anyone in management as every Representative claims to be my personal account Manager.

I have been a T-Mobile customer for probably close to 14 years. I've always paid my bill on time. I've always had an unlimited data text and voice plan. All of a sudden they start throttling my bandwidth. So evidently T-Mobile doesn't have an idea what unlimited data usage means. I will be shopping for a new Carrier and throwing away a 14 year relationship with T-Mobile.

edits.text: One has to ask the confidence level of a company who puts their bill in upside down envelopes and who doesn't understand what unlimited means. I have an unlimited plan yet I keep getting warnings about data usage and the fact that my band will be throttled down so obviously T-Mobile is too f***ing stupid to understand what unlimited means.

text: After purchasing my Tmobile 5G, received a $50 MasterCard which clearly mentioned you can use it everywhere. I added that card to my amazon wallet and tried to purchase  2-3 times. Every time card was declined due to insufficient balance. When I reached customer service, they transferred the line to the associated bank. the bank sent me the list of failed transactions and said, to fill up the form and fax us. we will investigate and it will take 45-60 days time. So this is poor service. I am not willing to continue and argue for $50. Asking T-Mobile not to provide this kind of promotional zero-balance card to customers.

Unhelpful. Rude. Dishonest. Don’t go with T-Mobile. Definitely don’t go to the Marshfield WI location. Some of the worst interactions I’ve ever had, in my life. They’re trying to get a commission, not help people. Very unhappy to be stuck with them in a two-year contract.

After 20 years of t-mobile service, I cancelled due to some life changes - spoke to rep when I cancelled and said my bill was caught up and I wouldn't need to pay anything to cancel. 

Well after cancellation I got a bill for 100 dollars.. Which wasn't my plan total so not sure where it came from- they sent me to collections, cause i missed the notice in the mail.. My fault I get that, but sorting it out hasn't been simple

Called to solve it and they couldn't verify me in any other way than a pin I set up at some point in the 20 years I was a customer.. 

Any spoke to a few people in customer service and they told me there was no other way to verify me outside of going to a t-mobile store. 

I get fraud is a thing, but they aren't trying to get my bills cancelled for me.. that's for sure.

During my attempt to file a complaint, they hung up on me.
If I had to give them even one star it's too many T-Mobile is the worst Internet data I've ever experienced I would never recommend them waste of money

I purchased a T-mobile phone and contract because of their reputation as a military friendly company and due to their assurance that our new phones would work in the area we were in. The service has been terrible. We have had dozens of dropped calls and areas of no service this last month, sometimes within blocks of the local T-Mobile store. I am locked in a two year contract so I can’t switch providers yet. I feel like I was lied to and taken advantage of by T-Mobile for an expensive phone plan that is basically useless much of the time. I also think that they are taking advantage of military service members by selling a product, they know doesn’t work, right next to the Navy Base where I live.

I was a Sprint customer for over a decade and was then pushed into this bumbling T-Mobile situation. My Sprint bill was always a low monthly rate and I had good experience,  then I was basically given very little option then to move  over to the T-Mobile plan I HAD to get a new phone. Now my monthly payment slowly increased by $30.00 per month, those disingenuous scabs will nickel and dime you to death and then still bill you while your in the ground!  The phone T-Mobile offered was free yes however not one store in America had one in stock..hmmmmm how convenient! So I'm now paying for a phone instead of being comped for their supply issues, knowing they were going to have a influx of Sprint customers to accommodate.  On top of that I was two days late paying my last bill due to pneumonia so now my bill has gone up every month for now on to include the late fee per Raven at Customer Service (611). T-MOBILE IS NOT AMERICAN MADE!!!! I'm done!

Received two different phones and returned both. Paid activation on both and after return Tmobile refuses to refund activation fee. I don’t even have the phones anymore because they had been returned and received by Tmobile. Would not recommend this company for a cell phone provider . Been with company 20 years and they do this. Frustrating!
The worst company I ever had

I have been a T-Mobile customer for over 3 years now and while the network is alright the service the company provides is absolutely terrible. I've spent hours on the phone with them to address the most basic things. Just right now I'm traveling internationally and trying to get my phone unlocked. I have called over 10 times. Talked to 10 different agents with the same request and haven't gotten a single helpful response. Either I'm told they will resolve it in 24-48 hours with no resolution in spite of calling every 24 hours for an update. Or say they will transfer my call to their manager and disconnect or simply put me on a never-ending hold just for someone else to connect and have me talk through my issue again. I've spent over 7 hours on the phone with them with my device still locked. I'm on hold as I'm writing this.
If there is negative stars I would give it, cause T-Mobile is the worst

T-mobile internet went down on a regular basis, making my outdoor cameras go down.  Also, I was told that I could return the router to the store with the shipping label.  But, I was advised by the store employee that if I dropped the router off at the store, even with the shipping label, that I am still responsible for the replacement cost of the item.  Won't go back to T-mobile again. Bad product and worse customer service.  Too hard to work with.

Thank you to Erin, Nick and Josh at the TMobile in Chaska, MN on Pioneer Trail for making our Black Friday Shopping experience that we were dreading so absolutely Fabulous!  Upgrading our phones, adding new internet plus all of the other extras - earbuds, screen protectors, etc.  They each had the patience to answer all of our questions, explain what we needed to do and when, even giving us a follow up call.  I was so impressed that they greeted everyone as they came in to let them know they would be with them as soon as possible.  The multi-tasking that went on with that team is incredible.  Great Synergy! It takes time to do transfer the phones so set your expectations and patience.  Even after a they had a long day meaning worked all day and night, skipped lunch, full store they stayed until after close to make sure everyone's transactions were done.  We would highly recommend and will be back for all our future purchases.  Thank you all so very much!  Nick and Roxanne

TMobile's customer service has really gone downhill. We used to call in and get local people who dealt with things efficiently and understood everything we said right away. Now the reps have to ask several times for us to restate the issue... They continually misunderstand our statements. It's very frustrating. I want to change service but where do we go where they don't outsource customer service. The last rep actually lied to me and promised us a free s22 ultra for our "5 years as faithful customers" (I have screenshots) but when I called in to check on it they said the rep had mistated a promo... That we needed to add another line to receive the phone... Which the rep had never mentioned at all. We're so over this hassle with customer service that we're willing to pay more for good customer service but where would that be?
They commit fraud, they steal, they cannot be reached and do not react. Terrible!!

Worst experience EVER. I SENT A DEFECTIVE PHONE BACK WAS TOLD IT WAS RECEIVED IN THEIR WAREHOUSE AND I HAVE PROOF. NOW A MONTH LATER THEY CAN'T FIND THE PHONE AND ARE TRYING TO CHARGE ME FOR IT!!! WORST CUSTOMER SERVICE. LIES

I called to have a phone unlocked to I could use a new provider. I was told that he unlocked my phone and I could turn it off and back on and it would work.  After about 20 mintues of that not working I called back to find out that it takes up to 72 hours.  This is a business phone and it will leave my elderly clients without no way to contact me or their caregivers when they need help or if they have an emergency.  This WILL result in a major loss of bus for me.  I couldn't be more an disappointed in you.

Their service is not good. It's really isn't.  Then they add charges and I guess what I. Mostly upset about was all the money I spent on my phone and then they turn around and send me deals for the same phone for 500 less than what I paid and wouldn't give me any break on it. Just over them. I would rather pay more for better

They delivered my new phone to an auto zone across town so I canceled my order, they actually asked me to pick it up and deliver it back to them! This is where the fun begins! They continue to charge me for the phone that I never possessed for 2 months and offered to fix it all and give me $100 for my trouble. They applied the $100 to the phone (that I never possessed) and are now charging me for the entire phone (that they have and have record of  possession)! I've been with them for over 20 years and they do not care, I now owe them for a phone that I have never touched! I had to get a lawyer, do not ever go with tmobile

Worst customer service on the planet!  I purchased a refill card from Fry's grocery store and for some unknown reason when I tried to use it the transaction wouldn't go through.  To me the refill card appeared to be missing numbers.  I called and was told to take the card to the local T-Mobile store which I did and was very rudely told "not our problem."  So I went to point of purchase and the Fry's manager told me the same thing.  It appeared I was going to be out $40.00.  I started writing letters to both Fry's and T-Mobile.  The latter has not responded but Fry's did give me a refund for the $40.00 card for which I am grateful.  Not so happy with no response from T-Mobile who got their money but won't EVER get my business again.  It's time to get classy and buy an Apple phone.  Bye bye  T-Mobile.

edits.text: They deserve zero stars.  I used to think they were great and when I'd stop into the store they were pleasant and helpful.  Not so much anymore.  Maybe the employees are hoping to put them out of business so they can collect unemployment?  I recently purchased a refill card at Fry's and for some reason the card is faulty and unusable.  I took it back to Fry's with the receipt and they said it "wasn't their problem" and suggested I take it to T-Mobile.  So, today I tried to do that but not only was this woman? rude but they wouldn't honor the $40 I paid for a faulty card.  That's it for T-Mobile.  I'll be off to Best Buy to find another phone and most certainly another carrier. Very disappointing to see this company go downhill but after today's experience I hope they totally tank.  They are a despicable company.

text:
I was a t-mobile customer for over 10 years.  I had my account set up for auto pay and everything was fine.  I then had an issue with the bank account and had to close that account and move my autopay for the new account.  Well it has been hell.  I call the customer service and they tell me since I don't have some pin number which was never set up with the account, I couldn't change to the new account nor could I shut service off.  I didn't pay the bill and eventually the service was shut off.  T-mobile won't work with me on anything to prevent the issue, but was willing to send me to collection for a bill that isn't a lot.  
At the end of the day, I am glad to not be a part of T-Mobile and I encourage everyone to think before signing up for a service.  They have the worst customer service and all they want is money.  They can take my money over the phone, but can't look into anything on my account.  I have spend several months dealing with T-Mobile drama.  I work hard and don't want to sit and play their games.  Worst Worst Company Ever.

I was offered a promo credit of 1000 to trade in a phone for the new iPhone. I sent in my brand new S22ultra and got the new iPhone. Turns out, they don’t actually give a thousand dollars credit all at once, but spread it out over two years. This is sneaky and manipulative—it’s a way of ensuring that I pay the monthly phone bill for 2 years and if I leave before that I am responsible to pay the remaining balance on the phone. Come on guys! That is very low

New customer to receive virtual $200 gift card never received went into the local store  after 8 weeks and manager said its been escalated and told me to wait another 8 weeks.  Called customer service only to find that they  talk the talk but DON'T provide any solutions.     Customer service calls you back and leave messages but never call when scheduled.  I've talked to supervisors, managers with no solution.  Stay away as the customer service is extremely poor.

If I could I would give T-Mobile Internet a -10.  We went to T-Mobile store and picked up a modem.  We were thinking that we would switch companies.  My husband picked up modem and we spent the whole day trying to get a stronger signal.  We tried 27 windows and never got a stronger signal than WEAK.  We called customer service for help to no avail.  The next day husband returned modem to store as requested by Customer Service.  Two weeks later we receive a bill for $50.00 stating we are being turned over for collection.  We didn't have the modem for over 24 hours and never connected to the internet due to WEAK signal.  I called Customer Service and was told we have to go to the store to clear it up as they cannot help us because my husband couldn't remember 6 digit code he originally set up for the modem when he received it.  We thought it was done and finished when modem was returned, store gentleman told us we were good no bill will come, he fixed it.  FALSE.  When I called customer service, after she could not help me, I asked for a manager and was told she would notate our account but not speak to me.  What a wonderful, caring company that loves it's customers and is very helpful, NOT.  My husband is so frustrated over this.  We are both seniors and it is getting more and more companies seem to be about the money and not customer service.  Stay far away from their internet and I would never go to them for phone service.

If I could give them a negative star. I have been a customer of T-Mobile for a little over a year. They sold me a phone that’s not 5G capable and I have had nothing but issues with it since I got it. They want me to pay another gear for what they call a phone and what I will call an expensive paperweight because that is all it will be good. If you were trying to decide which phone carrier to go with, I would strongly advise you not to go with T-Mobile because with the numerous calls and going into locations they have done nothing to help this issue out.

After being a loyal and trusted T-Mobile customer for almost 20 years, I was travelling in Europe this past summer (2022) and using the Magenta plan that I've been on for years, where international roaming is included. I ended up staying significantly longer than intended, had no idea there was a time limit to the roaming, but in early October I got sent a text that informed me if I didn't stop using the international roaming by October 27, my account would be blocked for roaming. On October 6 I called T-Mobile and changed my plan to talk and text only, no roaming, removed my sim and used a local European one instead. A day and a half before my impending return to the US, I had my Magenta plan reactivated, but discovered the roaming feature was not working. I called T-Mobile, only to be told that because I hadn't stopped roaming within the time warned, my account had been blocked from roaming FOREVER. I had three separate calls where I explained in great detail every step I had taken, including switching my plan and removing my sim card well before the warning date, but no-one would help me! They said it was an irreversible system (AI) configuration and that the system had somehow picked up my phone was pinging cell towers (not possible!) and therefore registered that I had not stopped the roaming. In summary, a loyal and trusted customer of 20 years was treated like a criminal, all because of a mistake made by T-Mobile's faulty AI system. This company has completely lost track of who they started out as, a company who used to care for their customers and treat them as humans. No longer. So I left for Mint Mobile, a smaller, much more human, and cheaper option.
Currently a T-Mobile customer and have been for almost 10 years, This last year they have completely gone downhill! We are changing companies this month.

I'm upset.  I'm a veteran of 8 + years. The veterans administration had me free phone service through t-mobile for a long time. The funding for the free phones stopped, t-mobile let me keep my number, but now I have to pay 60 a month! No problem with that,  but now I have to go prove I'm a veteran. Just goes to show, that T-MOBILE never really cared enough about veterans in the beginning! They don't even know we been in their system!

I am a current T-Mobile customer, switching to Verizon. My wife and I received our new Verizon phones yesterday 11/19/22  and went into the store to have our numbers ported over from T-Mobile to Verizon. While my wife's phone number ported over just fine, mine did not. I was advised by the Verizon associate that T-Mobile had put a hold on my phone number until 11/21/22. No where in my agreement with T-Mobile does it state that a hold would be put on my number for transferring providers. I have transferred providers many times in my life and this has never happened.

Purchased a screen protector for my phone and had T-Mobile install it.  The protector retails for $40.  Within a few weeks it appeared to be coming off and the issues has only gotten worse day after day.  Called T-mobile and they said since I don't have insurance with them I should go directly to the company re the warranty.  Surprise, surprise there is no warranty and the GoTo brand appears to be a T-Mobile product.
T Mobile acts like a f***ING scam company. Ordered esim postpaid contract. No activation, no support customer service, cancellation number can’t help either, but they charged my credit card!!!!!!!

The 0.25 seconds I last in bed is longer then how long this Wi-Fi router stays on for. My wife is more satisfied by my terrible cooking skills than T-Mobiles horrible service. Nothing gets her wetter then them sending us a new router every week. 10/10 recommendation

Tmobile has gone down the tube!

Their customer service agents don't have basic knowledge of the plans and promotion offerings.   Have to ask the same question multiple times, and point out that they have not addressed my issue.   Asked to speak to a supervisor and the agent tried every tactic to not transfer me to their supervisor.

When I upgraded my phone at a Tmobile store, I was not given any promotions detail in writing, and later found out I had been misled on the promotion.  

Pathetic experience - I am an 8 year Tmobile customer and these are the worst experiences I have had with any company - for any type of product or service.

An absolute nightmare, very unprofessional company at all levels, its been 3 days that they charged me to transfer my lines from googlefi and wasted hours of my time making me hold on-line and they can't figure out how to do a simple transfer - run fast -  run away from T-Mobile  pay $40 extra to AT&T or Verizon believe me T-Mobile is  cheaper for a reason!

Absolute sh**.. If you think it will be comparable to any other major celluar company you are mistaken. They will charge you more and will not evem give you fast enough data to use a mobile hotspot to watch one (1) movie without it needed to load every15 minutes. Peice of sh**.
I thought Verizon was the worst but I see T Mobile is even worse. They are not helpful at all. They would rather let you go to another carrier then to save a current valued customer.

T mobile you suck.   Two of the 6 phone started having problems at one year of service   They are hardwear problems.  Phones have never been dropped  never had problems with Verizon Wireless but they keep increasing price.   Shop buying junk from China.

NEW N20 OCT.22,2022.  CALLED 3 TIMES.  NO COMPLAINT.  4TH CALL SUCCESS. RECVD. 11, 16,2022. T-MOBILE OFFICE 17TH W/ 2ND. SAME PROBLEM EXACTLY.  CALL NUMBER NO RECOG. 3 DIGIT EXTENSION. MUST PAY RESTOCK FEE ON BOTH AND PAY HALF BALANCE OF ORIGINAL PHONE.  POOR CUST. SERVICE.  WANT ME TO PAY FOR DEFECTIVE PHONES.

so unbelievably disappointed with the Customer service I received today from T-Mobile. I've been with T-Mobile for almost two years until today when T-Mobile abruptly cancelled by Mobile service without any notice. I received a message today from T-Mobile saying my mobile internet services has been cancelled. I immediately went into the T-Mobile store for help as their phone support is terrible. Once, in the store I ask for help in understanding why my services has been cancelled, and the mobile Rep, said we cancelled your services because of roaming, "you roam too much". I explained to the Rep that I travel a lot to care for my mom who lives out of state and he said well we send you messages in the past about roaming and you continue to roam.  I explained to the Rep that each time I received a message from T-Mobile with regards to my roaming, I would call T-Mobile asking what do I need to do on my end to resolve the roaming issue, and was told not once but THREE times, "Oh, there is nothing you need to do, you are fine, no worries and your services will not be disconnected". T-Mobile refused to re-connect my services today and pursue to tell me, " We will not re-connect you , and you will have to wait 90 to be reinstated with T-Mobile and told me that they will not release my mobile number for 90 days, I've had this number for over 15 years and I travel often, and was more then willing to pay the additional cost for roaming charges but was not NEVER offer the option.  The treatment I've received today from T-Mobile is beyond belief! I didn't know T-Mobile was such and a  horrible, Customer unfriendly, unprofessional company.

I am sorry, I thought ATT and Verizon were garbage, but WOW, T-Mobile is the most pathetic of them all. I called them to transfer my service from AT&T to T-Mobile. The agent who took care of the process provided me with every single wrong piece of information that he possibly could. To make the story short, now I am stock with a phone that doesn't work with AT&T or T-Mobile. My phone number is stuck in a limbo thanks to T-Mobile mess and I have to go to a store physcially figure out what to do.

First I'd like to say their customer service is good.  We dealt with Drew in the Walnut Street store in Philadelphia.  He was great.  As far as the switching over from another carrier...READ THE FINE PRINT.  They say if you switch you get a free IPhone 14.  Not true if you want a Pro or better.  ASK QUESTIONS!!!

My phone was supposed to be delivered today but was deemed lost in transit by UPS and I tried to get them to talk to UPS since they were technically the shipper and they basically told me nothing could be done for 7 whole days.
I either expect a refund of what I've already paid or something, this is unacceptable.
Terrible  keep losing  signal  don't do it

So I transferred two phone lines to AT&T cellular service on end Sep. The rep on the phone transferred the phone numbers but never mentioned once I had to tell them to deactivate the service even though once I transferred my number I no longer had access to my tmobile account. So I got billed for a whole other month that I didn't even use. It would of been nice if the representative would of explained this to me over the phone since it was my first time transferring a phone number. I was disappointed

T-Mobile will rip you off & mess up your credit, I have not had their services since the beginning of July did what their representative on a recorded line told me to do return the phone and I will owe nothing. Now I am receiving letters from a debt collector talking about I owe them money for an open account for September and I have not had their services since the beginning of July. Legal action needs to be taken against them, for telling lies and trying to get money because they are mad that nobody wants to pay for their high services anymore. I was with them for 8 years and this is how they do. Please people let's take legal action against T-Mobile for illegally charging false bills.

I want to congratulate Joseph (T mobil river st). Very polite and competent person. I live in Woburn, I've always been to Tmobil in Stoneham, but the employees there don't care much about helping customers. I almost switched to Verizon, I've been a Tmobil customer for over 15 years. But luckily I was served by Joseph, thank you very much for your attention and patience with customers.

I pay for insurance on the phone, and they send me an inferior phone to replace the phone: I have a motorola stylus 128 gb 5 g phone and they sent me a samsung galaxi A13 64 gb phone which is reviewed as being crap.

And now they want me to send the bad phone (samsung galaxi a13) back and then wait for them to recieve it and then call back again and then choose between 3 "available" phones 

and then choose between 3 models with T mobil and then ask the assurance  insurance company if that model is available.

And the strange part is that the motorolla stylus 5g that I pay insurance to replace is available anywhere except in Assurance wharehouse,....the insurance seems to be a scam:

I only need a screen and if I take the inferior model that they sent me, and then I need to send back the motorola , they could fix the screen for about 30 dollars and then sell the repaired model for 179 bucks within a week

Tmobile has continued to say we owe for things we've already paid for.  We paid for our devices and now they're tacking on a monthly charge for "equipment". Their response is that the phones were initially FREE, but if you switch providers, then they charge you for the phone!!  That is so misleading,"Join our phone service and we'll give you a FREE phone."  This is deception!  DO NOT switch to Tmobile, they will try to rip you off!!  There's no way to contact headquarters except by snail mail to a PO box.

I have been with this company for a few years now but Mostly I have gone into the store where the service is ok But I was unaware that their customers service area is overseas possible the Philippine or India.I ordered a phone on the team mobile app and this is where my headaches begins. So the phone was sent  ups to me and 3 attempt was made to deliver on the 3 I contact ups the day after as I returned home from work late evenings I was advise that I am unable to pick up device as my package was return to sender I contacted T-mobile customer service “ My assigned team” who gave me the runaround and ask me to contact ups again and again and I will get a call from T-Mobile 2 hours later. I contacted ups and got the same answer package return to sender. i am still waiting for that phone call. I call t-mobile again and the rep told me that  my package/device was delivered to me at 4am central time. In a time zone that I don’t live in she was horrible and took me out of character. I ask to speaks to manager and he got on the phone and advise me that the device was returned to sender and he will contact the warehouse  and call me 48 Horus later to let me know which ups I can go to to pick up package I’m am still waiting 72 hrs later. I Called again spoke to another no clue rep that told me my package is in Texas and she will give me the # for ups to pick up Note I reside in a whole different state the she told me if I can’t pick up the device not to worry my card will be credited and I can repurchase. I advise that she is not making any sense.I ask to speak to the manager that help me and I was advise that she can put give out information on the previous people I have spoken to. This is such a headache. If this is not resolve by T-Mobile I will be switching this is going in for 5 days now I cannot get an answer to how I can get my device. My coworker also switched from t-mobile for the same reason. I should have directly to the consumer instead of a third party vendor I thought it would be simple but this have increased my stress level. I still have no idea of when and how to get the devices I ordered online.  T-Mobile how is it that your customer service is so horrible don’t you want to keep your customers.

Can't send MMS, can't send text, can't receive phone calls. No refund offered, no apology. 

Take your money elsewhere.
F u c k t mobile the still mony

I have never experienced such unprofessional organization is this. I have always had to contact customer services and complain daily about services or billing etc. Their home internet lost signal often and even on cell service no internet. I would never recommend this company to anyone , cheap plans means cheap service you get what you pay for!!!!!!!!!!
The worst customer service and the signals doesn't work properly.

I have not had service for 2 weeks due to a faulty phone that is only a few months old and in warranty. Every time I go to pick up the replacement phone I am told tmobile system is down and to come back another day. So supposedly the system has been down for 2 weeks and they do not know when it will be back up. When you call customer support they are full of excuses and no one is able to help. Save your money and go somewhere else I currently have to use ATT's wifi for my Tmobile phone because the phone will only work on wifi.

I don't know if its just me but every time i call their customer service representatives never let me finish talking! They are so quick to try and get me off the phone instead of listening to my problem. My email is linked to another account so i cannot access the app and they keep saying that they are not able to unlink my email. I've told them this several times and they still tell me to go on the app! They do not care about their customers! Its outrageous how bad this is! I'm so fed up! You never actually get the help you need!

I have been with T-Mobile for 2 years, recently ordered the new iPhone was promised 2-3 day delivery. Phone shipped really quick and was “out for delivery” on Friday 11th with UPS. Driver showed up at my door with a package then refused to give it to me, said “oh this isn’t yours” took off, refused to let me see it. Thankfully it requires a signature which I didn’t give. Then suddenly the phone/ package disappears and no one knows where it’s at. After 5 days of calls and chats with UPS and t-mobile no one can help me. I’ve been hung up on, transferred and told “it’s in the way” I’m so frustrated! I bought an account through T-Mobile before going on my tour in Europe. Well I've been paying for insurance for the 6 months I've had been with T-Mobile I went in the very first time I told them I'm coming from boost Mobile here's my boost Mobile phone a minor standing I get a free phone when I because I signed up with you which I was agreed with come to find out he lied to me that was charged for that phone I didn't even get to pick the phone just whatever he handed me 1 then I lost that phone I went in and explained to them that I lost the phone I had insurance they went in the back room came out handed me a phone I didn't get to pick I've never done this before I didn't know they put it on the insurance then I lost that phone this time they went in the back room for a half an hour it came out hand me a phone I get home they replaced my 5G phone I lost with a 3G phone and when I look at my bill they charged me for every one of these phones knowing I had insurance and was under the expectation that insurance would cover it but the thing is the insurance company is a third party so if they put it on the insurance they don't make any money so they leased me every one of these phones had I known I was going to pay for him obviously I would have insisted I got to pick the phone I got which I never got to do not once and now they've got me over a barrel I can't leave them because I own them $500 for phones that should have been put on insurance I've never met anybody that was so foul I talked to the CEO of customer service apparently and the only thing she did was replace the 3G phone with a 5G phone that should have happened to begin with and did nothing about everything else that happened T-Mobile blows

Already closed account, horrible customer service.  I was with them for over 15 yrs. The last 3 yrs has had horrible customer service, messing up my bill and just the beginning of Nov, I e changed companies, followed porting protocol, closing account, obtaining pin (all phones,watches,etc paid off) and they call and charge me an exorbitant amount to close my account.  DON'T DO TMOBILE

Devices are supposed to be unlocked automatically after paying them off. If your device isn't able to be unlocked remotely, they are supposed to send you the unlock code automatically as soon as your final payment is made. Guess what? I've gotten 3 different answers about how long this takes to happen after you REQUEST it. My device was paid off months ago and I am having to jump through hoops to get it unlocked now. They don't even have it associated with my account, even though I ordered it through my account and had a payment plan for it on my account! Contact customer service and all they can do is put in an escalation ticket. You might get the code in 24-48 hours, 24-72 hours, or 5-7 business days (at least, these are the 3 different answers I've received so far). The customer service reps can't help you, can't track your ticket, can't get the unlock code for you, can't give you the number or transfer you to the "specialized team" that handles your request, and apparently can't see that you've contacted them about an issue recently (at least, not the reps I've talked to because I have to re-explain the situation every single time I call). No ticket number or anything. All of this for an unlock code that T-Mobile has and maintains and doesn't have to contact the manufacturer to get (I confirmed this for the device in question with Sonim, the manufacturer). I've had T-Mobile for more than 5 years with no late payments or bad standing ever. I never had any issues until after I had to request account closure because T-Mobile can't service Alaska phone numbers, which I need for business purposes. Signal is pretty good almost anywhere (even some locations in the oil field where hardly anyone has service), but the lack of CSRs who can do anything about issues like this or really give you information about it is quickly souring what was an otherwise good overall experience with the company while I have had service.

Worst service I have ever encountered. Difficult to understand costumer service due to lack of knowledge and back ground noise at call center. Worst customer service I have ever encountered. Talked to at least 8 different reps during these calls to be given different instructions each time, none of them were correct.

We have been good loyal customers of T-Mobile for a long time. My son has an I-phone 11 pro max which seems to keep freezing. We have taken it to T-Mobile and the service tech told me that it is because Apple changed the software and it can not be uploaded into these old phones. I told my son to go into a local T-Mobile store and trade the I-phone 11 for the new I-phone 14 to solve the software problem, since he lives 2 hours (one way) away from my home. He took the phone into a T-Mobile store in Greenville, North Carolina who's employees are very badly trained both on the phones and customer service. The store actually called me to get authorization, but told me that I needed to add my son to the account before they can help him. Now you may trust your kids in a T-Mobile store, but not me. I said to them why don't you tell me how much it's going to cost me for the new phone, since his phone is on my account. They told me that since I'm not at the store, they can't give me any information about pricing. I said then why do we have pin numbers that verify who you are talking to. When I call customer service that's the first thing they ask for so they can authenticate the person. Obviously, I don't drive to Texas to show them my ID. The store said "we'll that's the way we do things around here". Well, let me tell you, if you don't want to lose old loyal good customers like me, then you need to figure out how to handle this problem. My money spends just as good at other cellular companies which I'm sure they would love our business. Remember T-Mobile your Free Tuesdays aren't that good to keep me, if I don't get the customers service I pay for. I think since the last CEO left, you guys have gotten worse and worse. I think it is time for customers to BOYCOTT your company and leave. Then we will see yur customer service policy change.

TMobile has terrible customer service!! People buy because they like the product AND because the like the sales rep. Nasty sales reps = less sales. They need to train their sales people on customer service & customer support. Their phone service isnt that great and their rude.

My phone was part of a sim swap scam and I didn’t get any help from T-Mobile!! My mobile bank accounts and mobile wallet started having money just vanish. I owned a staffing agency and my employees pay would disappear from my bank Acct! I had to beg and borrow money to cover around 45k in missing  payroll at the last minute. My credibility was ruined. No one wants to work for a staffing agency that can’t pay them. So I closed! I had no clue what was happening!? Then I started getting texts from lending sites saying my carrier service had changed. My mobile bank had 5 different devices all labeled my I Phone all registered within a 48 hour period and so much more…. I called T-Mobile  and they told me that someone ported my phone number to AT&T!? What!!? They didn’t even notify me. They then said it must not have been successful because I was still connected. WRONG! I was still active in both somehow weeks after I reported it to them. I had to call AT&T myself!! So T-Mobile didn’t even look into it or try to help me. I called every day for over 60  days and every day someone would promise me a solution and then something would happen to the phone call or I would get transferred to someone else and get a different result! I couldn’t even get a discount or a new phone line. Customer service treated me like I was a criminal or I was crazy. Then my Apple IDs password and pin was changed and my phone was completely disabled for six to seven weeks. I thought I would lose it after that! Knowing all of this…. T-Mobile wouldn’t offer me a phone to use or a new number…instead I was hounded about my bill and treated as if I were nuts.  All of my accounts registered with my phone number were taken over and emptied and T-Mobile allowed it to happen. They tried blaming. AT@T but I was their customer!  They were not worried about securing my data and privacy! They only cared about my money. I am -10k in my bank account, -5.5k in my cash app, and -450 in Apple Cash and they only cared about me paying my bill. They even told me they wouldn’t help me until I had paid it. Look at how much money they cost me!! Not to mention losing my business and all of my employees losing their jobs. T-Mobile should be shut down until more secure and safe procedures are in place. Customer service needs to have more options to give people who call them for help and to not treat victims as if they are the criminals.

I tried their 5G Internet service for 15 days, nice service, but it would not meet my needs, after 14 days I cancelled and was not to be charged the monthly fee.  Yesterday they hit my bank account for the $50 monthly fee.  I had already closed the account and returned the equipment.  I called customer service about the issue, and one hour later the representative called me back.  A company as big as T-Mobile does not have any way to write me a check for the money they stole.  Best option according to them is go to your bank and dispute the charge.  Interesting that they didn't have any trouble taking $50 from my closed account....NEVER AGAIN.

Horrible, inquired about service was sent a Sim card. Never activated it. T mobile ended up putting a fake number in to the account in order to start charging me payments. I did not agree to this. After 4 months of charging me 85$+tax, I went to the store to get my money back. After it was not in my account after 2 weeks I called. The informed me I'd need to wait a month. Month went by npthing. Same thing again I called was told to wait a month, still nothing. Was told to go to the t-mobile store again and put in a pin and while I was there they processed half of the money I paid (2 months worth). I called them today and they told me my pin is incorrect and told me I'd have to go back AGAIN to the store. I'm so fed up with this. I've been trying to get my money back now for 4 months. If you value customers service and not getting scammed go to a different provider. This is the worst company I have ever dealt with and I'm not even a customer

 Sadly, my previous provider had 5-day outage, so tried T-Mobile. insane that such a company would have such horrible service. Even more insane, they seem to be my best option in my area, so I have to keep and hope for improvement!!

Always dropping calls. When I call my parents. Who I put on my plan since it's them two. We can't call eachother without interface. Always drops calls. Says no network. Absolutely annoying! Worse company ever.

T Mobile customer service is horrible…spoke with numerous representatives about my bill being incorrect and the make notes and state that they will correct the issue concerning my promotions not being applied…claiming they will have a manager resolve this issue and the manager doesn’t seem to know what’s going on and doesn’t take the time to read or research the history of my complaints. Manager attempt to claim there may have been a problem with the device instead of the promotion fishing for reasons not to correct the issues.

I visited the T-Mobile store at 4717 Colleyville Blvd. today  and thanks to Mr. Jonte Porter, the newly appointed Store Manager had the worst customer service experience I have ever had. He threatened me and my wife  with eviction from the store and finally ended up apologizing to us for his behavior when another customer at the store (who had witnessed his behavior) intervened and told him in no uncertain terms that he was way out of line and he needs to learn the basics of customer service.

In signing me up  with T-Mobile  (3 lines on the Magenta 55+Plan) on October 31 the  T-Mobile associate  made a mistake which was going to cost me $1200 if not corrected.  I went to the store today to follow up on a "ticket" that the store had written up to correct the problem.  Mr. Porter (who joined as  the  store manager on November 1) told us that he was the only person in a position to resolve the issue but that he could not talk to us because he was on his lunch break (it was 4:30 pm) and that we should come back in one hour. He also refused to allow anyone else in the store to help us. 

T-Mobile should assess if Mr. Jonte Porter  is  fit to be a store manager -- I don't think so.  I strongly urge others to think twice before visiting this  store, especially if he is still around as the store manager. I wonder if this is what T-Mobile's service is all about. It has me worried. Ironically, T-Mobile had charged me $105 (for 3 lines) for customer support as a part of the sign up process (so that I could experience Jonte Porter's  version of "customer service"!).

I had a prepaid plan with esim but I changed my phone and the esim didn't work anymore. They couldn't help me. I spent 2 hours calling them but at the end I needed to pay again. The support team tries hard but for some reason, it doesn't work. Such a pity.

I've been with T-Mobile for many years and the only reason I'm still with them is my wife kept getting new phones that we need to pay off.  I've put my foot down and she will be stuck with her phone until we are done paying it off.

Over the years I've had several experiences of T-mobile not removing a service that I requested to be removed.  I turned off a tablet data service that stayed on for a few months until I had to call in repeatedly until I saw it was finally off.  I had a similar experience with phone insurance which is rip off.  You pay like $10 more everyone month and if you break your phone or need a replacement, you still pay a lot of money for the replacement.  If you never use it, it's money flushed down the toilet; they won't even credit you any part of it to upgrade your phone.

Recently I noticed an increase in my bill and the website mentioned that I added something.  I called to find out what it was and they said i was an automatic start up of insurance on an iPad I had for over a year.  I had to call them twice after telling them that evening to remove the insurance since I never authorized it to be added.

It's shame that after years of being a loyal customer that they won't do a thing to offer any kind of free or reduced upgrade.  I can't wait to pay off everything and go elsewhere.

T-Mobile's Connect plan is a good deal and the coverage is fine. The customer support is the worst on the planet. Seriously, words can't describe how bad it is. Their "experts" are sales guys. If you can get past them, you'll wind up talking to somebody in a call center somewhere in Asia who has no knowledge of cell phones. They will just read and repeat what's on their screens. Ask for a supervisor and you are on hold for half an hour. It's really amazing that a company so big can have such super-horrible customer support.

Been with T-Mobile 9 years 9 horrible years just got lied to about a phone said they wouldn't charge my credit card for the $50 would be reimbursed right away find out it's $170 and they did not reimburse it so stop the payment people stay the f*** away from T-Mobile

T-MOBILE - GOODBYE

My company and I have been loyal T-Mobile customers for as long as they have existed.  We currently have over 30 devices using T-Mobile.  My wife and I still own the company, a large specialty sub-contracting company which we remote into from home in Florida and when traveling in our RV.  Two days ago T-Mobile turned off the two hotspots that we travel with claiming that each went over it’s allotted data of 100gb.  Of course they were only turned on when we left on our trip so that’s over 200gb in 27 days while traveling.  I Googled how much that would be if it was all streaming Netflix (which we don’t do) and it’s 1gb per hour for standard definition or 3gb per hour for high definition. Seems like an impossible amount of streaming to do while driving or relaxing on the beach.  But maybe it did happen so I called T-Mobile to get my two hotspots turned back on.  After two hours and four customer service reps, including a supervisor, I was told the following:
1. There is no way we can turn them back on until the billing cycle completes.
2. You cannot pay us for the overage and get them turned back on.
3. We will not give you credits for the 7 months in 2022 that you paid us but didn’t use the hot spots (over $ 800).
4. You cannot roll over your unused data.
5. We do not have another plan we can offer you.
6. Maybe you should try using your cell phone as a hot spot.
7. I mentioned that I could easily put the Starlink satellite service on my motorhome with unlimited high speed data and they gave me a lecture on the difference between a hot spot and Startlink - which by the way costs about the same as what T-Mobile is charging for two hot spots.
8. After telling them because of their inability to solve my problem I was going to instruct my company to move our account elsewhere, they made zero attempt to keep our business.

So goodbye T-Mobile.  We cannot participate in company Zoom meetings nor upload and download company documents with no internet. We are taking our 33 devices and moving them to a provider that actually cares about our business and where customer service means what it says. How disappointing after all these years that nobody at T-Mobile is trained or given the authority to solve the customer’s problem.

I am posting this on my various social network platforms and I give my permission for anybody to share it. Maybe somebody at T-Mobile will see it and decide to change their customer service model.

PS - I am posting this from my iPad with 2 bars of an LTE signal.  It will be a long upload.

I have been a customer of Sprint for over 20 years. I used them for my business phones and for my private phones. Now that they have switched to T-Mobile, the customer service is the worst that I have EVER seen from ANY company!!! I have been incredibly tolerant of issues, having spent over 5 hours on the phone with numerous calls to solve an issue with voicemail not working. I have spoken to approximately 10 different people. I have been told that someone would call me back from the technical department. Nobody called me back. Nobody has been able to solve the problem. This problem has been going on for over 2 months now with no resolution. Each attempt has been a reiteration of the exact same questions without them looking at what has been done before. I began using Sprint soon after they entered the cellular market and have been a person who stayed even when service became bad for a while in the 90's. This is another level of incompetence never seen before. I can no longer use them and will move to another carrier.

This has got to be one of the worst Cell Phone carriers around.  When you talk online, it's all roses and they promise the world.   They do not tell you about the fine print you never see.  Swap the XR for a 13.  Well, they don't mention that after the 1st couple of months, they are going to start charging a fee for making the swap.   Lies, broken promises, and, more lies.  I only have 20 more months to go before I can dump this sorry group.
